expertScores.commentary: The debate converged to a highly actionable patch plan with exact title, abstract, section ordering, terminology changes, accounting caveats, trading-value stance, and a pasteable evidence ledger. The agents substantially challenged overclaiming around alpha, shoulder terminology, trigger diagnostics, and P&L boundaries, while keeping the final scope realistic for a short LaTeX document. The final package is directly implementable and avoids drifting into a broad research rewrite.
expertScores.bestIdea: Reframe the paper as forensic reconstruction plus in-sample hypotheses, not a proven alpha or profitability claim.
expertScores.weakness: The plan still depends on reporting actual overlay edge-distance statistics, but does not provide the computed values.

expertScores.commentary: The debate converged to a concrete, pasteable revision package with explicit title, abstract, section order, terminology changes, accounting caveats, and a corrected evidence-ledger table. The strongest work was narrowing the paper from an alpha claim to a forensic reconstruction with in-sample hypotheses. The main missed issue is that the agents did not verify against the actual LaTeX source, so some rename/move instructions may still require manual reconciliation with existing headings and dependencies.
expertScores.bestIdea: Reframe the document around observed versus inferred versus unproven claims, removing alpha from the title and treating trading value as inconclusive.
expertScores.weakness: The final package is strong conceptually but not source-verified, so it may still miss document-specific references, labels, or duplicated sections.

expertScores.commentary: The debate converged to a clear, directly applicable revision package with exact LaTeX replacement text, section order, terminology changes, and table layout. The strongest technical move was narrowing the paper from an alpha claim to a forensic reconstruction with in-sample hypotheses and explicit failure modes. The exchange also improved pasteability by resolving title language, evidence-ledger wording, overlay terminology, and the trading-value stance.
expertScores.bestIdea: Remove “Alpha” from the title and reframe the document around observed two-band LP behavior plus guarded, falsifiable hypotheses.
expertScores.weakness: The final plan still depends on reporting overlay edge-distance statistics that may not yet exist in the current document.

keyConclusions: All three experts judged the debate highly convergent, with identical scores: convergence 9, depth 8, feasibility 9, dynamics 8.

The final plan is considered directly actionable: lock the non-alpha title, replace the abstract, reorder sections, add accounting caveats, make trading value inconclusive, and use a pasteable evidence ledger.
The shared core conclusion is that the document should be reframed as forensic reconstruction plus guarded in-sample hypotheses, not proof of alpha, profitability, or a tradable signal.
unresolvedDisagreements: There is no major scoring disagreement; the experts differ mainly on the chief residual risk. Pragmatist and Architect emphasize missing overlay edge-distance statistics, while Devil's Advocate emphasizes lack of verification against the actual LaTeX source.
bestIdeas: Remove “Alpha” from the title and reframe the paper around observed two-band LP behavior plus falsifiable hypotheses.
Use an observed/inferred/not-established evidence ledger to keep claims about Binance tracking, trigger behavior, EOA role, and tradable alpha properly bounded.
weaknesses: The plan requires reporting actual overlay edge-distance median/range, but those values are not supplied and may not yet exist in the document.
The final patch was not checked against the actual LaTeX source, so headings, labels, duplicated sections, and internal references may still need reconciliation.
synthesis.recommendation: Apply the final revision package, but first inspect the source for surviving alpha/shoulder language and section duplicates. Compute or remove the overlay edge-distance reporting convention rather than leaving it as an unsupported placeholder.
